title: Registry Publication, SBOM, Provenance, Vulnerabilities, Policies, and CI Release · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Sbom Attestation
summary: A regulated service needs evidence for what an image contains, how it was built, which base it used, who approved it, and how rollback selects immutable content. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker sbom attestation increment in signed and policy-gated image release packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage.
Publish least-privilege authenticated content, verify digest after push, handle immutable tags, retention, mirrors, rate limits, and failure without credential leakage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example candidate has a mutable tag, incomplete SBOM, minimal provenance, critical scanner finding, and unsigned exception. Bind source, base, builder, attestations, signature, policy, and promotion to a digest. Competency probe: Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage. A defensible solution applies Docker Sbom Attestation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to signed and policy-gated image release packet.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-supply-chain-registry-release-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-supply-chain-registry-release-2
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker supply chain registry release 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      source: reviewed-commit
      base: approved-digest
      output: immutable-registry-digest
      sbom: spdx-attestation
      provenance: max
      vulnerability-policy: severity-and-exploitability
      signature: identity-bound
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release && docker scout cves candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject tag-only promotion, SBOM-as-vulnerability-scan, unsigned attestations, ignored base drift, or severity alone without reachability and exception ownership.
    repair: Bind source, builder, inputs, output digest, SBOM, provenance, signature, scan policy, exception expiry, and promotion evidence.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An SBOM proves every component is safe, licensed, used, and free of vulnerabilities. This fails the Docker Sbom Attestation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage.
